channel.title: yellow3 lab - Insights
channel.description: Perspectives on AI and what it makes possible. Writing from yellow3 lab on autonomous software, the future of digital products, and what European businesses need to understand about AI right now.

item.title: The gatekeepers are not protecting art. They are protecting the gate.
item.description: Cannes, the Grammys, the Oscars and SAG-AFTRA drew the same line on AI inside a single award cycle. The reasoning is about creativity. The structure underneath is about who gets to decide what counts.
item.media:description: A split scene at a film festival gate. On the left, stern institutional figures in black stand behind closed iron gates inscribed with the words "The gatekeepers are not protecting art. They are protecting the gate," with brass plaques naming Cannes, the Grammys, the Oscars, SAG-AFTRA and Bandcamp. On the right, a diverse young crowd streams through the open gate carrying cameras, laptops and tablets, beneath a marquee reading "Stories belong to everyone" and a banner for the World AI Film Festival.

item.title: The autopilots will not build themselves
item.description: Sequoia named the trillion-dollar prize: the next great company will sell the work, not the tool. But Bek's framework skips the harder question - who actually builds the autopilots, and how. The layer underneath the autopilot economy is where the real position is.

item.title: The Pioneers Are Not the Winners
item.description: Netscape created the browser. Google captured search. Today's AI pioneers are repeating the same pattern. A note for executives and boards on the difference between creating a category and capturing one.

item.title: MCP + A2A: The two protocols quietly becoming the operating system for AI agents
item.description: MCP and A2A are now the open protocol layer for agentic AI, both governed by the Linux Foundation. Here is what they do, why they matter, and what European business leaders should do about them in 2026.

item.title: The AGI Clock Is Ticking. What Should Founders Actually Do About It?
item.description: The AI 2027 report mapped a path to AGI with unsettling precision. Whether the timeline is 2027 or 2032, the strategic question for founders is the same - and the window to act is already closing.

item.title: The AI Continent Action Plan: A Founder's Guide to What Actually Matters
item.description: The EU just built the most ambitious AI infrastructure plan in European history. Here is what business leaders, founders, and enterprises need to know - and how to use it. A yellow3 lab analysis.

item.title: The Gun Just Fired for European Founders. EU Inc. Is Here.
item.description: EU Inc. lets any European founder incorporate across 27 countries in 48 hours for under €100. The starting gun just fired. Here is what it means and what comes next.

item.title: Who Is AI Really Coming For in Europe?
item.description: 202 million EU workers across 40 occupation groups, scored for AI exposure. Clerical and business professionals face the highest risk. Interactive treemap built on real Eurostat employment data and ISCO-08 task analysis by yellow3 lab.

item.title: Europe's 26 Million SMBs Have an AI Problem
item.description: DESCRIPTION: Only one in five European businesses uses AI. Nearly half are experimenting but almost none are operationalizing it. An analysis of 26.1 million SMBs, the adoption gap, and what the next 24 months will bring - based on Eurostat, OECD, Gartner, and European Commission data.

item.title: The Billion-Dollar Bet Against Language
item.description: Yann LeCun just raised $1.03 billion to prove that large language models are a dead end. AMI Labs, world models, and what happens when a Turing Award winner bets against the entire industry.

item.title: The Click That Launched a Thousand Exploits
item.description: The first autonomous AI cyberattack targeted 30 organizations with 80-90% of operations executed without human intervention. What GTG-1002 reveals about the future of agentic systems - and why builders need guardrails now.

item.title: The Architecture Problem
item.description: Every major AI agent platform is built on the same flawed assumption: that the LLM should be in charge. It shouldn't. Here is why the dominant architecture of the AI agent wave is heading for a reckoning - and what comes next.

item.title: China's Quantum OS Is Out. The West Hasn't Noticed Yet.
item.description: China just released the world's first open-source quantum computer operating system. It's free, it runs on any hardware, and nobody in the West is talking about it. They should be.

item.title: McKinsey Has 25,000 Employees You Will Never Meet
item.description: McKinsey's CEO now counts AI agents as employees. 25,000 of them. The world's most prestigious consulting firm just told you exactly where business is heading - and most leaders missed it.

item.title: The New Scale
item.description: The most dangerous competitor in your market probably works alone. No office. No team. No funding round. Just AI, leverage, and a founder who decided that small is no longer a limitation.

item.title: The Best Time to Be Human Is About to Begin
item.description: Everyone is asking what AI will take from us. Nobody is asking what it is about to give us. The answer might be the most optimistic story of our generation.

item.title: The Software That Fired Its Own Manager
item.description: Autonomous agents don't ask for permission. They ask for goals. The uncomfortable truth about what happens to the layer between strategy and execution when software can handle both.

item.title: The DPP Report Executives Actually Need
item.description: A 17-page executive briefing on Digital Product Passports. Verified deadlines, sector-by-sector obligations, data washing risks, and a practical action framework.

item.title: The Shopify Moment for Business Software Is Here
item.description: Before Shopify, building an online store required a developer and months of patience. The same shift is happening right now with business software - and the consequences for SMB growth are enormous.

item.title: I Analyzed What 50 AI Platforms Actually Cost to Build
item.description: A 788x price gap between AI models. $250/mo vs $2.3M/mo to build. A Copenhagen founder breaks down the real economics of AI platforms in 2026.

item.title: Europe's AI Slumber
item.description: The US controls 74% of global AI supercomputer capacity. Europe holds 4.8%. A European founder asks: are we regulating ourselves into irrelevance?

item.title: The Rise of Autonomous Software
item.description: Software that doesn't wait for instructions. The architecture, the implications, and the businesses it will create.

item.title: Your Software Should Work While You Sleep
item.description: The shift from tools that assist to software that acts. What autonomous systems mean for how businesses operate.
